mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are non-negative in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization nearing peaks, prompting equity markets to price in imminent corporate margin expansion and yield positive forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during late-cycle phases correlates with negative or flat 20-day SPX returns, or the signal loses predictive power when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly translates to stronger corporate earnings fundamentals, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week as markets adjust to revised earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 310 while policy rates remain below 2.0%, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting central bank tightening expectations that depress equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in > 60% of periods meeting the inflation and rate threshold, or the signal shows zero predictive IC.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during a monetary tightening cycle signals weakening consumer demand ahead of rate hikes, causing equity markets to discount future earnings and produce negative forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines during tightening cycles correlate with positive 5-day SPX returns, or the regime-specific predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.
